description: When introducing transitional CSS token aliases during a doctrine refactor (old --brand-* names → new semantic --civic-blue/--press-red tokens), define the retirement gate (mission/phase that removes the aliases) inline in the source file as a comment, not just in companion docs. Otherwise aliases silently outlive their purpose.
example: MCP-0 branding.css aliased 7 old --brand-* tokens to new doctrine tokens for backward compat with M08-R-era components. Initial commit had only a doc-side retirement plan (style sheet). Improve step added an inline CSS retirement-gate comment naming MCP-7 cross-page audit. Without that inline marker, an MCP-1+ agent reading branding.css would not see the gate.
graduation_note: freq=1; CC-local for now. Domain-general candidate — applies to any phased token migration in any vault. Re-evaluate at MCP-7 if pattern recurs across other Civic Press missions.

description: Token aliases inherit only the color, not the semantic relationship. When old palette had a pair (text-color, bg-color) that passed WCAG, re-aliasing both tokens to new palette values almost always breaks the contrast pair — even when each individual token is well-chosen. Audit ALL text-on-bg pairs after a palette refactor; do not assume aliases preserve a11y.
example: MCP-0 aliased --brand-accent (was gold #D4A843) → press-red #C8321C and --brand-primary-dark (was dark green #1A3009) → civic-blue-depth #1F3A52. Each token individually passes contrast on paper. But the CTABanner brand variant used these two TOGETHER (civic-blue-depth text on press-red button) — that pair went from 7.5:1 to 2.2:1, failing WCAG AA. Caught by axe-core on 4 pages. Fix: changed button text to --paper (4.8:1 on press-red, AA pass). Also applied to inline style on home hero CTA.
graduation_note: freq=2 across CTABanner.astro brand variant + index.astro hero CTA (same semantic pair, different surface). Domain-general — any token-system refactor that retargets aliases should run axe-core BEFORE mission close, not after. Graduation candidate for III canonical web-design pack — likely affects any vault doing palette swaps.
